content_en:
"And We have made from water every living

They ask, how is it that the resource of life does not provide nutrition to the body?

They In addition add that the thirsty person would regain his strength and activity when he satisfies his thirst. He will In addition be able to restrict his food intake and rely on water. However, the thirsty person would not quench his thirst if he eats a large amount of food. They add, "We do not deny that water helps transfer the food to the various parts and organs of the body. We only deny the statement that water does not have any nutritional value at all."

Another group denied that water is nutritional saying that water alone does not sustain life, or help the body grow, or restore the moisture that the body has lost through heat. However, their opponents do not deny these’ facts. They only say every substance provides nutrition in

~——— The Prophetic Medicine

a different way compatible to its nature. For instance, good aroma provides a type of nutrition, and this fact makes the nutritional value of the water even more apparent.

In short, when the water is cold and mixed with a sweet substance, such as honey, raisins, dates or sugar, it will be one of the most beneficial substances that enters the body and will indeed preserve health. This is why the best drink to the Prophet b was cold and sweet. Warm water begets flatulence and is not favorable for the body or the health.

Since water that is kept overnight is more favorable than recently drawn water, the Prophet ate said when he entered a garden of Abu At-Taihan:

"Do you hove any water that was kept in its skin overnight?"

He was brought some of that water and he drank from it. [Abu Dawood, bin Majah and Ahmad]. This Hadith was collected by Al-Bokhary, who narrated that the Prophet as said:

"If you have some water still in its skin (then bring me some). Otherwise we will sip (from the well).."

Water that is not freshly drawn is similar to yeasty

dough, while freshly drawn water is similar to the dough that does not contain yeast. When water is soaked overnight, its earthly and sandy pollutants will rest on the bottom. It was mentioned the Prophet as liked kept water, as 'A'esha said:

"Fresh water was being brought to the Messenger of Allah b drown from the well designated for drinking.”

The water that is kept in skins, especially leather skins, has a better taste than water kept in clay containers. This is why the Prophet “s asked for the water that was kept in skin containers rather than the other types of containers. When the water is kept in skin containers, it will filter through the pores in the skin [thus making its taste better]. Similarly, the water that is kept in clay containers that allow the water to filter is better than that in the clay containers that do not allow the water to filter. The Prophet s& had the best methods, the most honorable soul and the best guidance in every matter. He has showed his nation the most beneficial methods and means concerning the benefit of their hearts and bodies in this life and the Next Life. ,

Avesha said that the best type of drink to the Prophet b was cold and sweet. This statement might indicate that the Prophet's drink contained fresh, sweet water drawn from wells and springs. Her statement might In addition indicate that he used to drink water that is

—— The Prophetic Medicine

mixed with honey or soaked dates and raisins. Or, her statement could carry both meanings, which is the correct opinion.

"if you have some water in its skin. Otherwise we will drink from its mouth."

It indicates that it is allowed to drink the water directly from the poo! or container with the mouth. Many doctors advise against this method and say that it is harmful for the stomach. It appears that the Prophet b did so only to demonstrate that it is allowed. Further, drinking with the mouth from the pool is harmful when one lays on his stomach and face, such as drinking from a river or a spring. Drinking with the mouth from an elevated pool of water while sitting up is not different from drinking using the hands,

The Prophet b guided his nation to drink while sitting down

He specifically commanded his nation not to drink while standing up, He In addition commanded those who drink while standing to vomit. Yet, he did drink while standing as is correctly reported of him.

Some people said that the Prophet's drinking while

standing overrules his command not to drink while standing. Another group said that his drinking while standing only demonstrates that this practice is only disliked not prohibited. Another group said that there is no contradiction between the two Ahadeeth. The Prophet i& drank while standing when he needed to do so, as he came to the well of Zamzam and was given a bucket of water that he drank from while standing.

Drinking while standing begets many ailments and does not quench the thirst, nor will the water settle in the stomach so that the liver transfers it to the rest of the body. In this case, the water will descend quickly to the stomach and will aggravate it, and the water will not be digested properly. However, if one does this occasionally, it will not harm him.

Drinking water in three separate breaths

Muslim narrated that Anas said that the Messenger of Allah b used to sip the water in three separate breaths and would say:

"This method quenches the thirst better and is more palatable and sanitary."

This Hadith indicates that the Prophet “s used to remove the cup away from his mouth, take a breath and then drink some more. In another Hadith, the Prophet b

—— The Prophetic Medicine

commanded that one should not breathe in the cup, but should move the cup away from his mouth and breath away from it. This method of drinking is very beneficial for quenching the thirst and more sanitary as the Prophet” has stated. When the water enters the hot thirsty stomach in intervals, the second sip of water will quench the thirst left by the first, and the third will quench the thirst left by the first two. In addition, this method is better suited for the temperature of the stomach, so as not to suddenly invade it with cold substances. In addition, when one drinks the water in one breath, it will only partially quench the thirst unlike when he drinks it in separate sips.

This method [that the Prophet * taught us] gives better results than drinking the water or liquid in one breath, since water might dissipate the instinctive heat or weaken it, thus spoiling the temperament of the stomach and the liver. Water might In addition beget many other ailments, especially for those who live in warm areas, such as Yemen and Hijaz, and especially during summer. Drinking in one breath is dangerous for such people because their instinctive heat is weak especially during hot weather, as we have stated.

The Prophet's statement, "It quenches the thirst better and is more palatable and sanitary," is similar to what Allah said:

"And enjoy it without fear of any harm." (4:4) 292

In addition, when one takes the drink in one breath, he might choke on it because of the large amount of the liquid. However, there is no fear from choking when one takes a breath while drinking. Further, when one takes a drink, the hot gases accumulating in his body will ascend from around the liver and the heart because of the cold water or liquid that is descending on the stomach. In this case, the water will come rushing down while the gases are ascending, causing flatulence and sometimes choking the person. One will not enjoy the drink in this case.

In addition, when cold water descends suddenly on the liver it will weaken it and cause a decrease in its temperature. However, when one takes the drink in separate sips, the liver will not lose its warmth and thus will not weaken. Similarly, when one pours water on a boiling pot, it will not decrease its temperature significantly,

At-Termezey narrated that the Prophet b said:

"Do not drink in one breath just as the camel does. Rather, drink twice and thrice, and mention (Allah's) Name before drinking and thank (Him) upon finishing."

Mentioning Allah's Name before drinking and thanking Him upon finishing has a significant effect in benefiting from the drink, enjoying it while fending off its

—— The Prophetic Medicine

harm. Imam Ahmad said, "When the food has four qualities, it will have become perfect: when Allah's Name is mentioned before having it, when Allah is thanked after finishing with it, when there are many hands to eat from it, and when it is from legal, pure sources."

Covering pots and closing water skins

Muslim narrated in his Sahih that Jabir bin Abdullah said, I heard the Messenger of Allah b say;

"Cover the pot and tie the knot of the water skin, for there is a day during the year in which a disease descends and falls in whatever pot that is not covered, or water skin, which is not tied, that it passes by."

This Hadith contains a type of divine knowledge that the doctors could never reach on their own. Laith bin Sa'd, one of the narrators of this Hadith, said, "NonMuslims know which day it is, in January, and they try to avoid its harm."

The Prophet b commanded that the pots be covered, even with a branch, so that one makes covering it a habit. In addition, when one covers the pot with a branch, crawling insects will not fall in the food but will pass over it on the branch.

Furthermore, the Prophet ga commanded that we mention Allah's Name when covering the pot to repel Satan and insects and animals.

Drinking from the mouth of the water skin is not allowed

——— The Prophetic Medicine

AI-Bokhary narrated that Ibn Ab'ass said:

"The Messenger of Allah b disallowed drinking from the mouth of the water skin.”

There are many benefits in this Hadith. For instance, when one drinks from the tip or the mouth of the water skin, he will breathe in it and will give it an offensive odor. In addition, water may descend strongly and harm the stomach. In addition, there might be an insect in the water that one is unaware of, and that might harm him if he swallows it. In addition, water might be polluted with harmful substances that will enter the person's stomach while he is unaware. In addition, drinking from the mouth of the skin fills the stomach with air and thus the water will not have sufficient space.
content_id:
"Dan Kami jadikan dari air segala sesuatu yang hidup

Mereka bertanya, bagaimana mungkin sumber kehidupan ini tidak memberikan nutrisi bagi tubuh?

Mereka juga menambahkan bahwa orang yang kehausan akan mendapatkan kembali kekuatan dan aktivitasnya ketika ia memuaskan dahaganya. Ia pun akan mampu membatasi asupan makanannya dan mengandalkan air. Namun, orang yang kehausan tidak akan memuaskan dahaganya jika ia makan dalam jumlah besar. Mereka menambahkan, "Kami tidak menolak bahwa air membantu memindahkan makanan ke berbagai bagian dan organ tubuh. Kami hanya menolak pernyataan bahwa air sama sekali tidak memiliki nilai gizi."

Kelompok lain menolak bahwa air bersifat bergizi dengan mengatakan bahwa air saja tidak dapat menopang kehidupan, atau membantu pertumbuhan tubuh, atau memulihkan kelembapan yang telah hilang dari tubuh akibat panas. Namun, pihak yang berseberangan tidak menolak fakta-fakta tersebut. Mereka hanya mengatakan bahwa setiap zat memberikan nutrisi dalam

~——— Ath-Thibbun Nabawi

cara yang berbeda sesuai dengan sifatnya. Misalnya, aroma yang harum memberikan sejenis nutrisi, dan fakta ini membuat nilai gizi air menjadi semakin jelas.

Singkatnya, ketika air bersifat dingin dan dicampur dengan zat yang manis, seperti madu, kismis, kurma, atau gula, ia akan menjadi salah satu zat paling bermanfaat yang masuk ke dalam tubuh dan akan benar-benar menjaga kesehatan. Inilah mengapa minuman terbaik bagi Nabi ﷺ adalah yang dingin dan manis. Air hangat menimbulkan kembung dan tidak baik bagi tubuh maupun kesehatan.

Karena air yang disimpan semalam lebih baik daripada air yang baru diambil, Nabi ﷺ bersabda ketika memasuki kebun Abu At-Taihan:

"Apakah engkau memiliki air yang disimpan dalam wadah kulitnya semalam?"

Beliau dibawakan air tersebut dan beliau meminumnya. [Abu Dawud, Ibnu Majah, dan Ahmad]. Hadith ini juga diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari, yang meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Jika kamu memiliki air yang masih dalam wadah kulitnya (maka bawalah kepadaku). Jika tidak, kami akan meminumnya langsung (dari sumur)."

Air yang tidak baru diambil serupa dengan

adonan yang mengandung ragi, sementara air yang baru diambil serupa dengan adonan yang tidak mengandung ragi. Ketika air dibiarkan semalam, kotoran tanah dan pasirnya akan mengendap ke dasar. Disebutkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ menyukai air yang disimpan, sebagaimana 'Aisyah berkata:

"Air segar dibawakan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ yang diambil dari sumur yang dikhususkan untuk minum."

Air yang disimpan dalam wadah kulit, terutama kulit hewan, memiliki rasa yang lebih baik daripada air yang disimpan dalam wadah tanah liat. Inilah mengapa Nabi ﷺ meminta air yang disimpan dalam wadah kulit daripada jenis wadah lainnya. Ketika air disimpan dalam wadah kulit, air akan menyaring melalui pori-pori kulit [sehingga rasanya menjadi lebih baik]. Demikian pula, air yang disimpan dalam wadah tanah liat yang memungkinkan air menyaring lebih baik daripada air dalam wadah tanah liat yang tidak memungkinkan penyaringan. Nabi ﷺ memiliki metode terbaik, jiwa paling mulia, dan petunjuk terbaik dalam setiap urusan. Beliau telah menunjukkan kepada umatnya metode dan cara paling bermanfaat menyangkut kebaikan hati dan tubuh mereka di dunia dan di Akhirat.

'Aisyah berkata bahwa jenis minuman terbaik bagi Nabi ﷺ adalah yang dingin dan manis. Pernyataan ini mungkin menunjukkan bahwa minuman Nabi berupa air segar dan manis yang diambil dari sumur dan mata air. Pernyataannya mungkin juga menunjukkan bahwa beliau biasa meminum air yang

—— Ath-Thibbun Nabawi

dicampur dengan madu atau kurma dan kismis yang direndam. Atau, pernyataannya bisa mengandung kedua makna tersebut, yang merupakan pendapat yang benar.

"Jika kamu memiliki air dalam wadah kulitnya. Jika tidak, kami akan meminumnya langsung dari mulut wadahnya."

Hal ini menunjukkan bahwa diperbolehkan meminum air langsung dari kolam atau wadah dengan mulut. Banyak dokter tidak menyarankan metode ini dan mengatakan bahwa hal itu berbahaya bagi lambung. Tampaknya Nabi ﷺ melakukan hal itu hanya untuk menunjukkan bahwa hal tersebut diperbolehkan. Selain itu, minum dengan mulut langsung dari kolam berbahaya ketika seseorang berbaring tengkurap dengan perut dan wajahnya menghadap ke bawah, seperti minum dari sungai atau mata air. Minum dengan mulut dari kolam air yang lebih tinggi sambil duduk tegak tidak berbeda dengan minum menggunakan tangan.

Nabi ﷺ membimbing umatnya untuk minum sambil duduk

Beliau secara khusus memerintahkan umatnya untuk tidak minum sambil berdiri. Beliau juga memerintahkan mereka yang minum sambil berdiri untuk memuntahkannya. Namun, beliau sendiri pernah minum sambil berdiri sebagaimana yang diriwayatkan dengan benar dari beliau.

Sebagian orang berpendapat bahwa perbuatan Nabi ﷺ minum sambil berdiri membatalkan larangannya untuk minum sambil berdiri. Kelompok lain berpendapat bahwa beliau minum sambil berdiri hanya untuk menunjukkan bahwa hal ini hukumnya makruh, bukan haram. Kelompok lain lagi berpendapat bahwa tidak ada pertentangan antara kedua hadith tersebut. Nabi ﷺ minum sambil berdiri ketika beliau perlu melakukannya, seperti ketika beliau datang ke sumur Zamzam dan diberi seember air yang beliau minum sambil berdiri.

Minum sambil berdiri menimbulkan berbagai penyakit dan tidak memuaskan dahaga, serta air tidak akan mengendap di lambung sehingga hati dapat memindahkannya ke seluruh tubuh. Dalam hal ini, air akan turun dengan cepat ke lambung dan akan mengganggunya, dan air tidak akan dicerna dengan baik. Namun, jika seseorang melakukan ini sesekali, hal itu tidak akan membahayakannya.

Minum air dalam tiga tarikan napas terpisah

Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa Anas berkata bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa meneguk air dalam tiga tarikan napas terpisah dan bersabda:

"Cara ini lebih memuaskan dahaga, lebih sedap, dan lebih sehat."

Hadith ini menunjukkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa menjauhkan cangkir dari mulutnya, menarik napas, kemudian minum lagi. Dalam hadith lain, Nabi ﷺ

—— Ath-Thibbun Nabawi

memerintahkan agar seseorang tidak bernapas ke dalam cangkir, melainkan menjauhkan cangkir dari mulutnya dan bernapas menjauh darinya. Metode minum ini sangat bermanfaat untuk memuaskan dahaga dan lebih sehat sebagaimana yang telah dinyatakan Nabi ﷺ. Ketika air masuk ke lambung yang panas dan haus secara bertahap, tegukan kedua akan memuaskan dahaga yang tersisa dari tegukan pertama, dan tegukan ketiga akan memuaskan dahaga yang tersisa dari dua tegukan pertama. Selain itu, metode ini lebih sesuai dengan suhu lambung, agar tidak tiba-tiba diserang oleh zat yang dingin. Ditambah lagi, ketika seseorang meminum air dalam satu tarikan napas, hal itu hanya akan memuaskan sebagian dahaga, berbeda dengan ketika ia meminumnya dalam tegukan-tegukan terpisah.

Metode ini [yang diajarkan Nabi ﷺ kepada kita] memberikan hasil yang lebih baik daripada meminum air atau cairan dalam satu tarikan napas, karena air dapat memadamkan panas instinktif atau melemahkannya, sehingga merusak temperamen lambung dan hati. Air juga dapat menimbulkan berbagai penyakit lainnya, terutama bagi mereka yang tinggal di daerah panas, seperti Yaman dan Hijaz, dan terutama pada musim panas. Minum dalam satu tarikan napas berbahaya bagi orang-orang tersebut karena panas instinktif mereka lemah terutama saat cuaca panas, sebagaimana yang telah kami sebutkan.

Sabda Nabi ﷺ, "Cara ini lebih memuaskan dahaga, lebih sedap, dan lebih sehat," serupa dengan apa yang Allah firmankan:

"Dan nikmatilah ia tanpa rasa khawatir akan mudarat." (4:4) 292

Selain itu, ketika seseorang meminum minuman dalam satu tarikan napas, ia mungkin tersedak karena banyaknya cairan. Namun, tidak ada kekhawatiran tersedak ketika seseorang menarik napas saat minum. Lebih jauh lagi, ketika seseorang minum, gas-gas panas yang menumpuk dalam tubuhnya akan naik dari sekitar hati dan jantung akibat air dingin atau cairan yang turun ke lambung. Dalam hal ini, air akan turun dengan deras sementara gas-gas sedang naik, menyebabkan kembung dan terkadang membuat orang tersedak. Seseorang tidak akan menikmati minumannya dalam kondisi ini.

Selain itu, ketika air dingin turun tiba-tiba ke hati, hal itu akan melemahkannya dan menyebabkan penurunan suhunya. Namun, ketika seseorang minum dalam tegukan-tegukan terpisah, hati tidak akan kehilangan kehangatan dan dengan demikian tidak akan melemah. Demikian halnya ketika seseorang menuangkan air ke panci yang sedang mendidih, hal itu tidak akan menurunkan suhunya secara signifikan.

At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Janganlah kalian minum dalam satu tarikan napas seperti unta. Melainkan, minumlah dua atau tiga kali, dan sebutlah (Nama Allah) sebelum minum serta bersyukurlah (kepada-Nya) setelah selesai."

Menyebut Nama Allah sebelum minum dan bersyukur kepada-Nya setelah selesai memiliki pengaruh yang signifikan dalam mengambil manfaat dari minuman tersebut, menikmatinya sekaligus menangkal mudarat-nya.

—— Ath-Thibbun Nabawi

Imam Ahmad berkata, "Ketika makanan memiliki empat kualitas, ia akan menjadi sempurna: ketika Nama Allah disebut sebelum menyantapnya, ketika Allah disyukuri setelah selesai, ketika banyak tangan yang makan darinya, dan ketika berasal dari sumber yang halal dan suci."

Menutup panci dan mengikat mulut kantong air

Muslim meriwayatkan dalam Sahih-nya bahwa Jabir bin Abdullah berkata, aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda;

"Tutuplah panci dan ikatlah mulut kantong air, karena ada satu hari dalam setahun di mana suatu penyakit turun dan jatuh ke dalam setiap panci yang tidak ditutup, atau kantong air yang tidak diikat, yang dilaluinya."

Hadith ini mengandung jenis ilmu ilahi yang tidak pernah bisa dicapai oleh para dokter dengan sendirinya. Laith bin Sa'd, salah seorang perawi Hadith ini, berkata, "Orang-orang non-Muslim mengetahui hari apakah itu, di bulan Januari, dan mereka berusaha menghindari bahayanya."

Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan agar panci-panci ditutup, bahkan dengan ranting sekalipun, agar seseorang menjadikan menutupnya sebagai kebiasaan. Selain itu, ketika seseorang menutup panci dengan ranting, serangga yang merayap tidak akan jatuh ke dalam makanan melainkan akan melewatinya di atas ranting tersebut.

Lebih jauh lagi, Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan agar kita menyebut Nama Allah ketika menutup panci untuk mengusir setan serta serangga dan binatang.

Minum dari mulut kantong air tidak diperbolehkan

——— Thibbun Nabawi

Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Ibnu Abbas berkata:

"Rasulullah ﷺ melarang minum dari mulut kantong air."

Ada banyak manfaat dalam Hadith ini. Misalnya, ketika seseorang minum dari ujung atau mulut kantong air, ia akan mengembuskan napas ke dalamnya dan akan memberikan bau tidak sedap padanya. Selain itu, air dapat mengalir dengan deras dan melukai lambung. Di samping itu, mungkin terdapat serangga di dalam air yang tidak disadari seseorang, dan hal itu dapat membahayakannya jika ia menelannya. Selain itu, air mungkin terkontaminasi dengan zat-zat berbahaya yang akan masuk ke dalam perut orang tersebut tanpa ia sadari. Di samping itu, minum dari mulut kantong air memenuhi lambung dengan udara sehingga air tidak akan memiliki ruang yang cukup.

commanded that one should not breathe in the cup, but should move the cup away from his mouth and breath away from it. This method of drinking is very beneficial for quenching the thirst and more sanitary as النبي ﷺ” has stated. When the water enters the hot thirsty stomach in intervals, the second sip of water will quench the thirst left by the first, and the third will quench the thirst left by the first two. In addition, this method is better suited for the temperature of the stomach, so as not to suddenly invade it with cold substances. In addition, when one drinks the water in one breath, it will only partially quench the thirst unlike when he drinks it in separate sips.

This method [that النبي ﷺ * taught us] gives better results than drinking the water or liquid in one breath, since water might dissipate the instinctive heat or weaken it, thus spoiling the temperament of the stomach and the liver. Water might In addition beget many other ailments, especially for those who live in warm areas, such as Yemen and Hijaz, and especially during summer. Drinking in one breath is dangerous for such people because their instinctive heat is weak especially during hot weather, as we have stated.

النبي ﷺ's statement, "It quenches the thirst better and is more palatable and sanitary," is similar to what الله said:

"And enjoy it without fear of any harm." (4:4) 292

In addition, when one takes the drink in one breath, he might choke on it because of the large amount of the liquid. However, there is no fear from choking when one takes a breath while drinking. Further, when one takes a drink, the hot gases accumulating in his body will ascend from around the liver and the heart because of the cold water or liquid that is descending on the stomach. In this case, the water will come rushing down while the gases are ascending, causing flatulence and sometimes choking the person. One will not enjoy the drink in this case.

In addition, when cold water descends suddenly on the liver it will weaken it and cause a decrease in its temperature. However, when one takes the drink in separate sips, the liver will not lose its warmth and thus will not weaken. Similarly, when one pours water on a boiling pot, it will not decrease its temperature significantly,

At-Termezey narrated that النبي ﷺ b said:

"Do not drink in one breath just as the camel does. Rather, drink twice and thrice, and mention (الله's) Name before drinking and thank (Him) upon finishing."

Mentioning الله's Name before drinking and thanking Him upon finishing has a significant effect in benefiting from the drink, enjoying it while fending off its

—— The Prophetic Medicine

harm. Imam Ahmad said, "When the food has four qualities, it will have become perfect: when الله's Name is mentioned before having it, when الله is thanked after finishing with it, when there are many hands to eat from it, and when it is from legal, pure sources."

Covering pots and closing water skins

Muslim narrated in his Sahih that Jabir bin Abdullah said, I heard رسول الله ﷺ b say;

"Cover the pot and tie the knot of the water skin, for there is a day during the year in which a disease descends and falls in whatever pot that is not covered, or water skin, which is not tied, that it passes by."

This الحديث contains a type of divine knowledge that the doctors could never reach on their own. Laith bin Sa'd, one of the narrators of this الحديث, said, "NonMuslims know which day it is, in January, and they try to avoid its harm."

النبي ﷺ b commanded that the pots be covered, even with a branch, so that one makes covering it a habit. In addition, when one covers the pot with a branch, crawling insects will not fall in the food but will pass over it on the branch.

Furthermore, النبي ﷺ ga commanded that we mention الله's Name when covering the pot to repel Satan and insects and animals.

Drinking from the mouth of the water skin is not allowed

——— The Prophetic Medicine

AI-Bokhary narrated that Ibn Ab'ass said:

"رسول الله ﷺ b disallowed drinking from the mouth of the water skin.”

There are many benefits in this الحديث. For instance, when one drinks from the tip or the mouth of the water skin, he will breathe in it and will give it an offensive odor. In addition, water may descend strongly and harm the stomach. In addition, there might be an insect in the water that one is unaware of, and that might harm him if he swallows it. In addition, water might be polluted with harmful substances that will enter the person's stomach while he is unaware. In addition, drinking from the mouth of the skin fills the stomach with air and thus the water will not have sufficient space.
